questions.question: Why does the loader need a name map?
options: Pretrained tensors and local parameters share the same name
Published GPT-2 weights use names like wte, wpe, h.N.attn.c_attn; the local model uses tok_embed, pos_embed, blocks.N.attn.qkv; the loader translates one onto the other
questions.explanation: The two naming conventions are different histories; the map is a literal dict expanded per layer.

questions.question: What is the conv1d transpose, and which tensors need it?
options: Published GPT-2 stores c_attn, c_proj, and c_fc weights in tensorflow conv1d layout, which is the transpose of what nn.Linear.weight expects; the loader calls .t() on those during assignment
questions.explanation: Three suffixes need transposing; biases and LayerNorms are already shaped correctly.

questions.question: Why is the LM head not in the safetensors file?
options: It is computed at runtime from logits
Weight tying: the LM head shares storage with the token embedding (wte), so loading wte and aliasing lm_head.weight = tok_embed.weight after the load reconstructs the head
It is loaded from a separate file
questions.explanation: Setting lm_head.weight = tok_embed.weight after wte lands restores the alias; copying instead doubles the parameter count.

questions.question: What does the loader do on a shape mismatch?
options: Records the mismatch in LoadReport.shape_mismatch and refuses to assign, leaving the local parameter untouched
questions.explanation: Half loaded models are silent failure machines; refusing to assign keeps the report honest.

questions.question: Why is the demo's stub fixture not a real GPT-2 download?
options: Real files are not in safetensors format
The demo must run offline and quickly; the stub uses the exact pretrained naming convention so the loader code path is identical, and swapping in a real file changes nothing about the loader
questions.explanation: Same code path, smaller payload; the loader works on either.

questions.question: Why generate a sample before and after the load?
options: If the post-load samples are identical to the pre-load samples, the load did not change the model, which means the mapping silently missed every tensor
To warm up the GPU cache
questions.explanation: Sanity generation is the cheapest gate: a sample fingerprint that fails to change exposes a broken load that the LoadReport summary would not catch on its own.
